results.possibleIdentity: Howard H. Manko (often cited as H.H. Manko) was a prominent engineer and scientist specializing in metallurgy and metal joining techniques, particularly soldering. During the 1960s, he served as the Director of Solder Research and Development for Alpha Metals, Inc., which was headquartered at 600 Route 440 in Jersey City, New Jersey. Manko is widely recognized as a leading authority in the field of electronic interconnections and is the author of the seminal textbook "Solders and Soldering: Materials, Design, Production, and Analysis for Reliable Bonding," first published in 1964. His work significantly influenced industry standards for soldering and cleaning in electronics manufacturing. In 1974, he founded Manko Associates, a technical consulting firm based in Teaneck, New Jersey. Throughout his career, he was a frequent lecturer and contributor to technical journals such as "Insulation Circuits" and "Plating." He received several industry honors, including the IPC (Institute for Printed Circuits) President's Award for his contributions to solderability testing. His potential association with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) is consistent with the participation of many New Jersey-based industrial engineers in the organization's collaborative projects between the late 1960s and early 1970s.
